idea-plugin.name: Hot Path Heatmap
idea-plugin.description:
Static hot path heatmap for PHP and JavaScript/TypeScript.

Why? In source, every call reads as equally cheap: $user->getId() looks exactly like $user->calculateTheMeaningOfLife() — yet one is a trivial field access and the other may fan out through services, loops, and a repository. Hot Path Heatmap was built to make that difference visible at a glance, so you notice the expensive call before it becomes a production problem.

It highlights method call sites that may trigger expensive downstream execution paths — deep call chains, nested loops, repository / HTTP-client access, high fan-out — by statically composing cached method summaries from the IDE's PSI and indexes.

Each flagged call gets a severity-colored outline, a cost score in the gutter, and a tooltip explaining why. This is a static estimate, not a runtime profiler — it exists to give an early visual warning: "this innocent-looking line may be expensive."
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.16
  • Added a Sensitivity setting (Low / Medium / High) that controls how expensive a call site must be before it's flagged. Higher presets raise every band cutoff so fewer, only-costlier call sites light up. The previous thresholds are now the "Low" preset; the new default is Medium, so the heatmap is less noisy out of the box.
0.1.15
  • New icon set: refreshed plugin icon and toolbar toggle (on/off) icons.
0.1.14
  • Minimum IDE is now 2025.3. The toggle now uses the non-deprecated daemon-restart API (the old form was deprecated in 2025.3), so the plugin no longer reports a deprecated-API usage on 2025.3+. (If you are on 2024.2–2025.2, stay on 0.1.13.)
0.1.13
  • Made the "very high" score clearly distinct from "high": "high" is now a definite orange and "very high" a pure red, so the top two severity bands no longer look alike.
0.1.12
  • PHP and JavaScript are now optional dependencies. The plugin installs in any IntelliJ-based IDE (not only PhpStorm) and activates whichever of the two languages is present. This fixes the "binary incompatible — package com.jetbrains.php not found" error reported when verifying against IDEs that don't bundle PHP (e.g. IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm).
0.1.11
  • Added back an inline marker: a bold straight underline on the call name (distinct from the wavy underlines IDE diagnostics use), alongside the gutter score. It is emitted on the lowest highlighting layer, so any warning/error on the same token always takes precedence — the marker is suppressed there rather than doubling up or painting over the diagnostic.
0.1.10
  • The estimate now shows only as a colored score badge in the editor gutter — the inline marker on the code is gone. With nothing painted on the code text, the gutter number can use an intuitive warm yellow → orange → red heat ramp without ever colliding with IDE warnings or errors.
0.1.9
  • Call-site marker is now a purple background "heat tint" that deepens as the estimated cost rises — the literal heatmap look. It uses a single purple hue and no underline/box, so it never collides with IDE warnings or errors (which use wavy underlines and yellow/red).
0.1.8
  • Call-site marker is now a full boxed outline, and the palette moved to a cool blue → magenta → purple ramp (no warm yellow/orange/red tones, which read as warnings).
0.1.7
  • New call-site marker: a dotted underline instead of a solid box, so it no longer reads as an error. The severity ramp now tops out at purple (amber → orange → magenta → purple) rather than an alarming red.
0.1.6
  • Fixed call sites in TypeScript files being analyzed and painted twice (doubled gutter score and outline). TypeScript is now handled once, via its JavaScript base language.
0.1.5
  • Added a plugin icon (shown in the Plugins list and on the Marketplace).
0.1.4
  • Exclusion now follows the IDE's own directory marks instead of matching path strings: folders marked Excluded and Test Sources Root (via Mark Directory as…) are skipped by default, each toggleable in settings. Built-in/library code is always skipped.
  • Aligned the toolbar flame icon with the Run/Debug controls.
0.1.3
  • Added a show/hide toggle button to the main toolbar (next to Run/Debug), also available in the Tools and editor right-click menus. A lit flame means the heatmap is on; a slashed flame means it is off.
  • While hidden, no cost scores are calculated at all.
  • Expanded the plugin description with the motivation behind it.
0.1.2
  • Removed the upper IDE-compatibility bound so the plugin installs on 2024.2 and all future PhpStorm builds. Verified compatible against the 2024.2 and 2025.3 (253) lines.
0.1.1
  • Listing metadata: license, vendor, and description updates.
0.1.0
  • Initial release.
  • Static hot-path heatmap for PHP and JavaScript/TypeScript.
  • Severity-colored marker, gutter cost score, and an explanatory tooltip.
  • Configurable heuristics, traversal limits, and vendor/test exclusion.

action.description: Show or hide the hot path heatmap. While hidden, no cost scores are calculated.
